events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection result directly to the requested JSON file. The observations given line up with typical PCC-1 flange assembly checks, so I’m encoding this as a compliant inspected joint with low residual risk.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"pass\",
  \"risk\": \"low\",
  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",
  \"component_conditions\": [],
  \"percent_complete\": 100,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [],
  \"actions\": [],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed 6-inch Class 150 flanged joint has the required full stud complement installed, hardened washers fitted, acceptable stud projection of one to three threads beyond the nuts, visible spiral-wound gasket matching the line class requirement, torque witness marks on every nut, and a documented star-pattern tightening sequence completed in three passes. These observations support compliance with engineered bolted-joint assembly practice at the tested/inspected stage.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"ASME PCC-1 bolted flanged joint assembly guidance: gasket verification, bolt/nut/washer installation, controlled multi-pass cross-pattern tightening, and post-assembly inspection/marking\",
    \"ASME B16.5 flange standard for 6-inch Class 150 flange configuration and bolting arrangement\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json && python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json"
